message: br-bd-af5t: pin fspick null path parity

 {"id":"bd-a892","title":"gethostbyname_r/gethostbyaddr_r clobber h_errno on ERANGE (glibc parity)","description":"Cod's bd-[-] fix (commit af29f73) fixed gethostbyname2_r to preserve h_errno on ERANGE (glibc contract: h_errno untouched when caller buffer too small). The same bug exists in two adjacent functions:

1. gethostbyname_r_impl (resolv_abi.rs line ~1558): on write_reentrant_hostent Err, unconditionally sets h_errnop=NO_RECOVERY_ERRNO and returns the code — clobbers h_errno even when code==ERANGE.

2. gethostbyaddr_r_impl (resolv_abi.rs line ~1628): identical pattern.

Fix: on ERANGE specifically, leave h_errnop untouched. Add tests mirroring cod's gethostbyname2_r_ipv6_small_buffer_preserves_h_errno.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T21:17:53.192254067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T21:20:47.242976502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T21:20:47.240761887Z","close_reason":"Fixed h_errno-clobber-on-ERANGE in gethostbyname_r_impl and gethostbyaddr_r_impl in commit 3089d44. Both now guard the h_errnop write by code != ERANGE, matching cod's gethostbyname2_r fix (af29f73). Renamed existing hardcoded-bug test to assert glibc-parity sentinel preservation; added gethostbyaddr_r_small_buffer_returns_erange_preserves_h_errno for the symmetric reverse-lookup path.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-a916","title":"Conformance: execute regex_glob_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for regex_glob_ops.json cases in the harness.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:47:08.628595578Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:50:51.086705966Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:50:51.084758248Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for regex_glob_ops fixtures and verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","regex-glob","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-abi","title":"Kernel: Proof-carrying policy tables (tests + perf)","description":"Validate policy table correctness and overhead.

Tests:
- Hash mismatch -> fallback.
- Table lookup determinism.
- Safety invariants preserved.

Perf:
- Lookup cost is O(1) and cache-friendly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:33:34.017248997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:20.395495161Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:10.604581606Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-abi","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-abi","depends_on_id":"bd-3kh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-abi","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1066,"issue_id":"bd-abi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: Added 12 new unit tests in policy_table.rs (lookup_from_artifact_roundtrip, lookup_default_cell_is_fast_allow, lookup_deterministic_same_inputs, lookup_all_families_all_modes_succeed, risk_bucket_boundaries, budget_bucket_bit_packing, consistency_bucket_thresholds, key_v1_index_distinct_for_different_inputs, hash_mismatch_yields_no_lookup, sha256_artifact_verifies_and_looks_up, mode_refinement_violation_rejected, lookup_with_overridden_cell). Added 3 integration tests in mod.rs (pcpt_hash_mismatch_falls_back_to_default, pcpt_lookup_deterministic_across_calls, pcpt_lookup_o1_cost_no_allocation). Total 18 policy_table unit + 9 kernel integration PCPT tests. 809 membrane tests pass. Perf baseline rebaselined.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-af5t","title":"syscall: fspick NULL pathname should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged fspick(AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0) fails with EPERM instead of a pointer-validation errno. Add ABI regression coverage to keep that observed privilege-first behavior explicit.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:44:00.769306489Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:44:00.769306489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-af5t","title":"syscall: fspick NULL pathname should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged fspick(AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0) fails with EPERM instead of a pointer-validation errno. Add ABI regression coverage to keep that observed privilege-first behavior explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:44:00.769306489Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:41:28.702732641Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:41:28.700499372Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-afqt","title":"vDSO classify_vdso_return: boundary + invariant coverage","description":"Cod's bd-1isd fix (commit 7440043) added classify_vdso_return() with 3 test cases (rc=7, rc=-EINVAL, rc=-ENOSYS). Missing coverage: (1) rc=0 Success path, (2) boundary rc=1 (smallest positive) + rc=-1 (smallest negative non-ENOSYS), (3) structural invariant: Fail(e) must always have e>0 since errno is positive. Adds quickcheck-style sweep + explicit boundary tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:49:36.638177405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:51:33.790524992Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:51:33.788466646Z","close_reason":"Added 3 complementary classify_vdso_return tests in commit 3b99cdc: Success pinning for rc=0, boundary rc=±1 and i32::MAX, errno>0 invariant sweep. Together with cod's 3 cases from commit 7440043, the classification function now has exhaustive coverage of all branch arms and the key structural invariant.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ahjd","title":"bd-2ry subtask: Deterministic fixture pack for nested/edge non-local jump scenarios","description":"Background:
- setjmp correctness must be demonstrated via integration fixtures, not just unit tests.

Goal:
- Add deterministic fixture pack for non-local control transfer, including adversarial edge cases.

Deliverables:
1) Fixture programs for nested jumps and error handling paths.
2) Strict+hardened expected-output profiles.
3) Failure artifact capture and triage docs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Fixture suite passes in declared supported scope.
- Any unsupported scenario fails with explicit documented semantics.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E scripts with deterministic replay controls.
- Structured logs: trace_id, scenario_id, mode, jump_depth, mask_state, outcome, timing, artifact_refs.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticCoast","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:53.128299308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T08:24:17.069464742Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T08:24:17.069446628Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic setjmp fixture pack + deterministic logging and triage artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","logging","setjmp","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ahjd","depends_on_id":"bd-2xp3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1067,"issue_id":"bd-ahjd","author":"RusticCoast","text":"Delivery evidence (RusticCoast):

Implemented deterministic fixture pack and triage docs for nested/edge setjmp scenarios:
- tests/integration/fixture_setjmp_nested.c
- tests/integration/fixture_setjmp_edges.c
- tests/conformance/fixtures/setjmp_nested_edges.json
- docs/setjmp/non_local_jump_triage.md
- scripts/check_setjmp_fixture_pack.sh
- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/setjmp_fixture_pack_test.rs

Deterministic artifacts generated:
- target/conformance/setjmp_fixture_pack.report.json
- target/conformance/setjmp_fixture_pack.log.jsonl
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-ahjd/trace.jsonl
- tests/cve_arena/results/bd-ahjd/artifact_index.json

Validation commands (all pass):
1) scripts/check_setjmp_fixture_pack.sh
2) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/frankenlibc-target-rusticcoast cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_fixture_pack_test -- --nocapture
3) scripts/check_setjmp_semantics_contract.sh
4) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/frankenlibc-target-rusticcoast cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test setjmp_semantics_contract_test -- --nocapture
5) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/frankenlibc-target-rusticcoast cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness setjmp_contract -- --nocapture

Notes:
- Added _POSIX_C_SOURCE guard in fixture_setjmp_edges.c for deterministic sigsetjmp visibility.
- This bead intentionally scopes to fixture/evidence infrastructure; runtime setjmp implementation work remains tracked by parent/dependent beads.","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:24:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-am9u","title":"EPIC: Dynamic System Constants — Replace hard-coded values with runtime queries","description":"FrankenLibC currently hard-codes several system-dependent constants (page size, stack limits, group counts, FD limits) that should be queried at runtime for cross-architecture correctness.

BACKGROUND: While FrankenLibC currently targets x86_64 Linux, these hard-coded values create latent bugs that will surface when porting to ARM64 (16KB/64KB pages), RISC-V, or systems with non-standard configurations. Even on x86_64, some values (like supplementary group count) can differ from defaults.

SCOPE: This epic covers all hard-coded constants identified in the comprehensive audit across malloc_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs, glibc_internal_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, pthread_abi.rs, and process_abi.rs.

ALREADY DONE: sysconf(_SC_PAGESIZE) now queries AT_PAGESZ from /proc/self/auxv at runtime (cached). The mmap_alloc bootstrap path intentionally keeps 4096 as the minimum alignment (documented).

RATIONALE: AGENTS.md says "We want to do things the RIGHT way with NO TECH DEBT" — hard-coded system constants are textbook tech debt.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PlumCove","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:22.060285167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:59.322247279Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:59.321965265Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
